text:
Billing Policy

1. Billing Cycle
All plans are billed in advance monthly or annually. The billing date is the same as the signup date.

2. Payment Methods
We accept credit card and ACH. Invoice payment is available for Enterprise plans with net-30 terms.

3. Failed Payments
We retry failed payments after 3 and 7 days. After 3 failures, the account is suspended. Restore within 14 days to avoid data deletion.

4. Invoices
Invoices are available in the billing portal. For duplicate or corrected invoices, contact billing@company.com within 30 days.

Customer Onboarding Policy

1. Scope
This policy applies to all new customers signing up for our services.

2. Onboarding Steps
- Account creation within 24 hours of signup.
- Welcome email with login credentials and quick start guide.
- Mandatory orientation call within the first 5 business days.
- First success check at 30 days.

3. Cancellation During Onboarding
Customers may cancel within the first 14 days for a full refund. After 14 days, standard cancellation policy applies.

4. Contact
For onboarding support, contact onboarding@company.com.

Incident Response Manual

1. Definition
An incident is any unplanned event that causes or may cause disruption to services.

2. Severity Levels
- P1: Full outage, multiple customers affected. Response within 15 minutes.
- P2: Significant degradation. Response within 1 hour.
- P3: Minor impact. Response within 4 hours.
- P4: Low impact. Response within 24 hours.

3. Response Steps
1) Detect and log the incident.
2) Assign severity and notify the on-call engineer.
3) Communicate status to stakeholders every 30 minutes for P1/P2.
4) Resolve and document root cause within 5 business days for P1/P2.

4. Escalation
If not resolved within SLA, escalate to the next level. See escalation_matrix.pdf for contacts.

Internal FAQ

Q: How do I process a refund?
A: Check refund_and_cancellation_policy. Within 14 days of purchase, full refund. After that, no refund for current period. Use the billing portal or email billing@company.com.

Q: What is the escalation path for P1?
A: See incident_response_manual and escalation_matrix. Notify on-call immediately; they escalate if not resolved in 30 minutes.

Q: Can we offer a custom discount?
A: Standard discounts are in pricing_rules_guide. For custom deals, get approval from Sales and document in CRM.

Q: Where is the onboarding checklist?
A: customer_onboarding_policy lists the steps. Orientation call within 5 business days is mandatory.

Pricing Rules Guide

1. Standard Plans
- Basic: $29/month, 1 user, 5 projects.
- Pro: $79/month, 5 users, 20 projects.
- Enterprise: Custom pricing, unlimited users and projects.

2. Discounts
- Annual billing: 2 months free (pay for 10, get 12).
- Non-profit: 20% discount with valid proof.

3. Add-ons
- Extra users: $15/user/month on Pro.
- Extra storage: $5/10 GB/month.

4. Price Changes
We give 30 days notice for any price increase. You may cancel before the change takes effect without penalty.

Refund and Cancellation Policy

1. Cancellation
- Written notice required at least 14 days before the next renewal date.
- Cancellation takes effect at the end of the current billing period.
- No partial refunds for unused time in the current period.

2. Refunds
- Full refund if cancellation request is received within 14 days of initial purchase.
- After 14 days, no refund for the current period. Prepaid future periods may be refunded at 50% if more than 30 days remain.

3. Exceptions
- Service failures lasting more than 72 hours may qualify for credit or refund. Open a ticket with support.

4. How to Cancel
Send an email to billing@company.com with subject "Cancellation" and your account ID. You will receive confirmation within 2 business days.

Service Level Policy

1. Uptime
We target 99.9% uptime for the Pro and Enterprise plans. Basic plan target is 99.5%.

2. Support Response
- Pro and Enterprise: 4-hour first response for standard tickets.
- Basic: 24-hour first response.
- P1 incidents: 15-minute response for all plans.

3. Credits
If we fail to meet the uptime SLA in a calendar month, you may request a service credit. Pro: 10% credit for 99.0–99.9% uptime, 25% below 99.0%. Enterprise: negotiated in contract.

4. Exclusions
Scheduled maintenance (announced 7 days in advance) and issues caused by customer actions are excluded from SLA calculations.

Technical Support Workflow

1. Ticket Creation
All support requests must be logged as tickets. Include: customer ID, description, severity, and steps to reproduce.

2. Triage
- First response within 4 business hours for standard tickets.
- P1/P2 tickets follow the incident response manual.

3. Resolution
- Use the knowledge base and internal FAQ before escalating.
- Document the solution in the ticket for future reference.
- Close the ticket only after customer confirmation.

4. Escalation
If the issue is not resolved within 2 business days, escalate to Tier 2. See escalation_matrix.pdf.
